title: terminalia habeensis
intro: Terminalia habeensis, also known as the "Haberjara" or "Black Pau", is a plant species native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. It is a deciduous tree with medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids in its bark, leaves, and roots. The medicinal properties of Terminalia habeensis have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, and can also be found in various Ayurvedic and Unani medicinal formulations.
